senses.gloss: obsolete spelling of مَا لِ (mā li)

examples.english: So what is with those who disbelieve...

senses.gloss: to bend, to incline, to slope
to incline, to be favorably disposed
to prepare to set (of the sun)
to come to an end (of the day or night)

etymology.text: Probably a univerbation of مَا (mā, “what”) + ل (l-, “to; belonging to”), as in مَا لِي (mā lī, “what belongs to me”). The root م و ل (m w l) then developed denominally. While this may seem like popular etymology, similar univerbations do exist in Arabic; compare particularly the dialectal verb جَاب (jāb). Compare also Classical Syriac ܡܵܠܵܐ (mālā, “property, wealth”).
